unverified_claims.note: The 36.6% CAGR cited by Business Research Insights is for the 2026–2030 forecast window, not a backward-looking 2021–2026 figure. The actual 5-year CAGR from 2021 to 2026 is estimated higher (~40–50%) given the explosive ChatGPT-driven demand inflection in 2022–2023. A single authoritative source providing a precise 2021 market baseline was not available. The 36.6% figure is cited as the best available documented CAGR proxy.

unverified_claims.claim: growth_3m: '+66% YoY' applied as 3-month momentum proxy
unverified_claims.note: The +66% YoY figure is NVIDIA's Q3 FY2026 (October 2025) data center segment growth year-over-year — not a sequential quarter-over-quarter figure. It is used as the most recent and authoritative short-term market momentum indicator. No single-quarter sequential growth figure for the overall AI chip market was available from a published source.

The 100,000 monthly search volume for 'AI chips' and related terms is a directional estimate. No primary keyword tool (e.g., Keywords Everywhere, Ahrefs) was directly queried. The estimate is based on the compound interest around searches for NVIDIA (millions/month), 'AI chip' (likely 50K–150K), and 'AI chip wars' (lower volume but trending). The actual volume may be higher if NVIDIA-adjacent searches are included.

The '35% of AWS AI workloads' figure from public sources refers to Amazon's internal deployment mix, not global market share. The 1–2% global market share estimate for Amazon Trainium/Inferentia is derived by applying AWS's ~10% share of global AI compute to the 15% fraction of AWS workloads running on Trainium. This is a rough estimate; no analyst has published a standalone Trainium/Inferentia global market share figure.

Huawei's market share is limited to China's domestic AI chip market due to US export controls. The 2% global figure is estimated from Huawei's ~4% of NVIDIA's compute throughput (CFR) and NVIDIA's ~80% share. Huawei's domestic China market share is much higher (~30–40% of China AI chip market), but the global figure remains small given China's reduced AI chip import access.
